title: podman pull error "no such host" on WSL2/podman-default-machine
body:
### Issue Description

I installed Podman for Windws11/WSL2 by winget.
Podman command worked, but pulling images had failure.

Default setting VM seems to lead  name solve error.
However, when I used VPN on Windows, this error didn't happen and podman could pull images.

I guess the cause is name solving problem, but no solution was found.

Please give me advice. Thank you.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. podman pull [image]


### Describe the results you received

> podman pull busybox
Resolved "busybox" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/busybox:latest...
Error: copying system image from manifest list: parsing image configuration: Get "https://production.cloudflare.docker.com/registry-v2/docker/registry/v2/blobs/sha256/52/5242710cbd55829f6c44b34ff249913bb7cee748889e7e6925285a29f126aa78/data?verify=1689320530-6VflZBDzdhIUa2Zuud23yde7Ayc%3D": dial tcp: lookup production.cloudflare.docker.com: no such host


### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected

### podman info output

```yaml
Podman: 4.5.2
OS: Windows11
podman-default-machine on WSL2
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
I was able to solve it myself by following procedure.

1. set `generateResolvConf = false` in /etc/wsl.conf
2. unlink /etc/resolv.conf
3. edit /etc/resolv.conf and write `nameserver 8.8.8.8`
4. `sudo dnf install e2fsprogs` for chattr
5. `sudo chattr +i /etc/resolv.conf` not to change by reboot
